# mockallofus

A local mock database for developing *All of Us* analyses without the
paid Researcher Workbench.

The [`allofus`](https://github.com/roux-ohdsi/allofus) R package wraps
access to the *All of Us* Research Program data, which lives in Google
BigQuery and is only reachable from the (metered) Researcher Workbench.
That makes iterative code development — especially for students who
rerun constantly — slow and expensive.

`mockallofus` builds a local [DuckDB](https://duckdb.org) database whose
tables and columns mirror the *All of Us* Curated Data Repository (OMOP
CDM core tables plus the *All of Us* extension and cohort-builder
tables), populated with **fully synthetic, randomly generated data**.
You can then develop and test the same `allofus` analysis code locally
that you would run on the Workbench.

> The synthetic data is random and **does not reflect real *All of Us*
> data** (which would violate the data use agreement). Results are not
> meant to be realistic — the point is to make code run.

## Installation

`mockallofus` currently requires the development version of `allofus`
from the
[`mock-duckdb-support`](https://github.com/roux-ohdsi/allofus/tree/mock-duckdb-support)
branch, which adds the backend-aware query execution that lets `allofus`
functions run against DuckDB rather than only BigQuery. (Once that work
is merged and released, the released `allofus` will work.)

``` r

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("roux-ohdsi/allofus@mock-duckdb-support")  # required allofus branch
pak::pak("louisahsmith/mockallofus")                # installs duckdb too
```

`mockallofus` declares this requirement in its `Remotes:` field, so
installing it (or its dependencies, e.g. in CI) pulls the correct
`allofus` branch automatically.

## Usage

The only thing that changes versus a Workbench script is the connection
call: use
[`mock_aou_connect()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_aou_connect.md)
instead of
[`allofus::aou_connect()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_connect.html).
Everything downstream is identical.

``` r

library(allofus)
library(mockallofus)
library(dplyr)

con <- mock_aou_connect()   # builds the database on first run, then caches it

# ...the same code you'd run on the Workbench:
tbl(con, "person") |> count() |> collect()

cohort <- tbl(con, "person") |> select(person_id)

aou_concept_set(cohort, concepts = c(201826, 4193704), domains = "condition",
                output = "indicator", concept_set_name = "t2dm")

aou_survey(questions = c(1585838, 1586135),
           question_output = c("gender", "birthplace"))

aou_observation_period()
```

### Making your study’s queries return data

A small default database means queries for *specific* concepts or
eligibility criteria can come back empty, which is harder to develop
against than no data at all. Rather than making the database enormous,
**seed** the concepts your study uses, at a prevalence you choose, so
eligibility joins return partial cohorts:

``` r

# guarantee these condition concepts exist for ~30% of participants
mock_seed_concept_set(con, concepts = c(201826, 4193704),
                      domain = "condition", prevalence = 0.3)

# guarantee responses to specific survey questions (real codebook concept ids)
mock_seed_survey(con, concept_ids = c(1585838, 1586135), prevalence = 0.6)
```

Seeding is deterministic (for a fixed `seed`) and attaches to existing
participants, so all downstream joins stay consistent.

## What works locally, and what doesn’t

Because most `allofus` functions are pure `dbplyr` (which translates to
DuckDB automatically) and the package’s internal query helper is
backend-aware, the large majority of workflows run unchanged.

**Works locally**

- `dplyr`/`dbplyr` on `tbl(con, ...)`,
  [`aou_join()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_join.html),
  [`aou_collect()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_collect.html)
- [`aou_concept_set()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_concept_set.html)
- [`aou_survey()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_survey.html)
  (with real codebook question concept ids)
- [`aou_observation_period()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_observation_period.html)
- [`aou_compute()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_compute.html),
  [`aou_create_temp_table()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_create_temp_table.html)
- [`aou_sql()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_sql.html) —
  **provided the SQL is portable**. BigQuery-only constructs (`COUNTIF`,
  `APPROX_QUANTILES`, etc.) will not run; a few common ones
  (`CONTAINS_SUBSTR`, `REGEXP_CONTAINS`) are emulated via DuckDB macros.

**Workbench-only (clearly-labeled exceptions)**

- [`aou_atlas_cohort()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_atlas_cohort.html)
  — relies on BigQuery-dialect SQL generated by `SqlRender`.
- File/bucket management
  ([`aou_ls_workspace()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_ls_workspace.html),
  [`aou_ls_bucket()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_ls_bucket.html),
  [`aou_workspace_to_bucket()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_workspace_to_bucket.html),
  [`aou_bucket_to_workspace()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_bucket_to_workspace.html))
  — these use Google Cloud Storage, which has no local equivalent.
- [`aou_session_info()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_session_info.html)
  CDR-metadata details.
- User-written SQL that uses BigQuery-specific functions.

## How it works

- [`build_mock_db()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/build_mock_db.md)
  creates every table in the *All of Us* data dictionary (from
  [`allofus::aou_table_info`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_table_info.html))
  and populates a core subset with synthetic rows. EHR-sourced rows are
  tagged in the `_ext` tables so EHR-based functions (like
  [`aou_observation_period()`](https://rdrr.io/pkg/allofus/man/aou_observation_period.html))
  behave correctly.
- [`mock_aou_connect()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_aou_connect.md)
  opens the DuckDB database, registers a few BigQuery-compatibility
  macros, attaches BigQuery-compatible `dbplyr` translations, and stores
  the connection in `getOption("aou.default.con")` and a schema name in
  `getOption("aou.default.cdr")` — exactly the options `allofus` reads.
- BigQuery-isms common in All of Us code are handled so pipelines run
  unchanged: the three-argument `date_diff(a, b, sql("day"))`,
  [`as.Date()`](https://rdrr.io/r/base/as.Date.html) of float-valued
  concatenated date parts (e.g. `"1994-5.0-28.0"`), and the
  `collect(page_size = ...)` download argument. More complex real-world
  pipelines (e.g. the HIPPS pregnancy algorithm) run end-to-end on the
  mock database after seeding their concept lists.

## Disclaimer

This project is not affiliated with or endorsed by the *All of Us*
Research Program. The data it generates is synthetic and does not
represent real participants.

# Package index

## Connecting

Connect to the local mock database. Stores the connection where allofus
looks for it, so allofus functions work against it unchanged.

- [`mock_aou_connect()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_aou_connect.md)
  : Connect to the local mock All of Us database
- [`mock_aou_disconnect()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_aou_disconnect.md)
  : Disconnect from the mock database and clear the stored connection

## Building the mock database

Create or locate the local DuckDB database.

- [`build_mock_db()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/build_mock_db.md)
  : Build a local mock All of Us DuckDB database
- [`default_mock_db_path()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/default_mock_db_path.md)
  : Default cache location for the mock database

## OMOP vocabulary

Optionally load the full OMOP vocabulary for concept-relationship work.

- [`mock_load_vocabulary()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_load_vocabulary.md)
  : Load a full OMOP vocabulary into the mock database

## Seeding your study’s data

Put the concepts and survey questions your analysis looks for into the
database, at a controllable prevalence, so queries return develop-able
data.

- [`mock_seed_concept_set()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_seed_concept_set.md)
  : Seed a concept set into the mock database
- [`mock_seed_survey()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_seed_survey.md)
  : Seed survey responses into the mock database

## Seeding building blocks

Lower-level primitives for inserting custom synthetic data.

- [`mock_add_concepts()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_add_concepts.md)
  : Add concept definitions to the mock database
- [`mock_add_occurrences()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_add_occurrences.md)
  : Add clinical-event occurrences to the mock database
- [`mock_person_ids()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/mock_person_ids.md)
  : Participant ids in the mock database

## BigQuery compatibility

Register BigQuery-compatible SQL on a connection (used internally).

- [`register_bq_macros()`](https://louisahsmith.github.io/mockallofus/reference/register_bq_macros.md)
  : Register BigQuery-compatibility macros on a DuckDB connection
